title: Visual Similarity Filter
description: Filters video frames by visual similarity using CLIP embeddings.
The first frame received becomes the reference; every subsequent frame
is scored against it and forwarded only if its similarity meets the threshold.
Includes pre-roll/post-roll context around matching segments.

input.description: A single reference image to match video frames against. The first image received on this lane sets the reference embedding.
Image frames to filter by visual similarity.

output.description: Frames visually similar to the reference image.
Similarity scores (frame index, timestamp, similarity) for matched frames.

vsf.similarity_threshold.title: Similarity threshold
vsf.similarity_threshold.description: Minimum cosine similarity (0.0–1.0) for a frame to be forwarded. Frames below this score are dropped.

vsf.fps.title: Input frame rate (fps)
vsf.fps.description: Frame rate of the upstream frame_grabber, used to compute timestamps in the output table.

vsf.reference_filename_pattern.title: Reference image filename pattern
vsf.reference_filename_pattern.description: Case-insensitive substring that identifies a reference image arriving on the image lane (e.g. 'reference'). Images whose filenames contain this string are used as the reference embedding; all others are scored as frames.

vsf.scene_change_threshold.title: Scene cut threshold
vsf.scene_change_threshold.description: Grayscale frame difference (0.0–1.0) above which a scene cut is detected and the tracker is reset. Once a car is confirmed in a scene, the tracker uses a lower similarity bar to keep forwarding it even when small or partially out of frame. Set to 0 to disable.
